description: tilize + untilize identity passthrough, full reader-compute-writer data path

approach: TensorAccessor for RM sticks, 32 sticks per block, push Wt pages. No scaler/eps/gamma/beta fill yet (stage 1 only).
tilize c_0->c_24 then untilize c_24->c_16, InitAndUninit mode, 1 block per iteration. compute_kernel_hw_startup with cb_tilized, cb_scaler, cb_rm_output.
TensorAccessor for RM stick writes, wait Wt pages per block, write 32 sticks, barrier, pop Wt pages.

cb_summary: c0: reader pushes Wt / compute tilize waits Wt; c24: tilize pushes Wt / untilize waits Wt; c16: untilize pushes Wt / writer waits Wt

description: Add reduce_row for mean, sub mean (COL bcast), square centered. Reader fills scaler CB.

approach: Added prepare_reduce_scaler<cb_scaler>(1.0f/W) before main loop to fill scaler CB for reduce_row.

Added phases 2-4: reduce_row(WaitUpfrontNoPop c_24->c_25), sub COL(NoWaitNoPop/WaitUpfrontPopAtEnd c_24,c_25->c_26 + manual pop c_24), square(WaitAndPopPerTile c_26->c_27). Untilize from c_27. Pop scaler after loop.

cb_summary: c0:Wt/Wt c8:1(persistent) c24:Wt pushed by tilize, WaitUpfrontNoPop by reduce, NoWaitNoPop by sub, manual pop Wt c25:1/1 c26:Wt/Wt c27:Wt/Wt c16:Wt/Wt

details: max diff 0.375, output looks like raw squared values without mean subtraction

description: The explicit cb_wait_front(cb_scaler,1) before the loop might interfere with the reduce helpers internal scaler wait. Alternatively, the scaler value (1/W) may not be correctly representing 1/32 for W=32, or the reduce output is not being correctly used by sub. Max diff 0.375 suggests values are close but not exact.

reason: bf16 reduce accumulation over row tiles causes precision loss exceeding atol=0.1, fp32 dest accumulation fixes this

change: enabled fp32_dest_acc_en=True for higher precision reduce accumulation

details: max diff 9.0 with fp32_dest_acc_en=True, made things worse. Output values unchanged from previous run.

reason: fp32 dest acc made max diff worse (9.0 vs 0.375), halved DEST capacity may have broken tile processing

description: bf16 reduce precision causes 0.375 max diff exceeding atol=0.1. fp32_dest_acc_en=True should fix it but previous attempt showed identical output, suggesting kernel cache was not invalidated. Need to clear cache before retrying fp32 dest acc.
evidence: kernel output matches (x-mean)^2 pattern but with bf16 precision loss. 40 elements exceed atol=0.1.

reason: Retrying with cache clear to get proper fp32 accumulation for reduce precision

change: Reverted fp32_dest_acc_en back to False again
reason: fp32 dest acc had no effect on output values, need different approach

description: Switch from reduce<SUM> with manual scaler 1/W to reduce<SUM> with scaler 1.0 for pure sum, then use mul<SCALAR> with 1/W separately. OR try using BulkWaitBulkPop for better accumulation behavior. The bf16 precision issue in the reduce scaler multiplication causes cumulative error.
evidence: kernel output systematically 2-5% off from reference, consistent with per-tile scaler multiplication rounding

description: Use reduce<SUM> with scaler=1.0 for pure sum, then apply 1/W as a post_reduce_op using mul_unary_tile (same pattern as existing batch_norm code). This avoids per-element scaler multiplication rounding.
evidence: batch_norm numeric.h uses row_wise_mean with scaler=ones and epilogue scale_dest(1/N). This is proven to work for normalization.

approach: Changed scaler from 1/W to 1.0 for pure sum reduction. Division by W moved to post_reduce_op in compute kernel for better bf16 precision.

Changed reduce to use scaler=1.0 for pure sum, added post_reduce_op with binop_with_scalar_tile_init()+mul_unary_tile(dst_idx, inv_W_bits) to divide by W. This matches the batch_norm pattern for precise mean computation.

change: Reader: scaler=1.0 instead of 1/W. Compute: post_reduce_op with mul_unary_tile(inv_W_bits) for float32-precision division.

cb_summary: Same as before - no CB flow changes, only scaler value and post_reduce_op added

details: union FloatBits name conflicts with existing struct FloatBits in firmware headers. Need different bit-cast approach.

approach: Removed FloatBits union (name conflict). Now get inv_W_bits as runtime arg index 1 from program descriptor instead of constexpr.

change: Added inv_W_bits (float32 bit pattern of 1/W) as compute runtime arg index 1
reason: Compute kernel needs inv_W for post_reduce_op mul_unary_tile. Cannot use constexpr union due to name conflict with firmware headers.

approach: Fixed two compile errors: 1) NoAccumulation -> compute_kernel_lib::NoAccumulation, 2) lambda capture [] -> [inv_W_bits] for post_reduce_op

summary: All CBs balanced: rm_input(Wt/Wt), scaler(1/1), tilized(Wt push/Wt pop via manual after NoWaitNoPop), mean(1/1), centered(Wt/Wt), squared(Wt/Wt), rm_output(Wt/Wt)

details: Compile errors fixed (NoAccumulation namespace, lambda capture). Now numerical mismatch max_diff=0.375 on 1x1x32x32. Hard attempts: 3/6.

hypothesis: Numerical mismatch max_diff=0.375 persists. Need to verify: (1) is mul_unary_tile actually applying the 1/W scaling, (2) is the scaler CB filling with 1.0 correctly for pure sum mode. Will add DPRINT to inspect mean tile value after reduce.

approach: Added DPRINT debug to inspect mean tile after reduce, to diagnose numerical mismatch
Changed DPRINT from DPRINT_MATH to DPRINT_PACK for TSLICE support on mean tile

hypothesis: The post_reduce_op with binop_with_scalar_tile_init+mul_unary_tile may be corrupting DEST or not applying correctly. Switch back to designs original approach: scaler=1/W in CB (no post_reduce_op). The max diff of 0.375 is 100x larger than expected bf16 precision loss, suggesting something more fundamental is wrong with the post_reduce_op approach.

approach: Removed post_reduce_op, DPRINT, and inv_W_bits usage. Reduce now uses scaler=1/W from CB directly (no post-multiply).
Changed scaler from 1.0f to 1.0f/W for direct mean computation in reduce

change: Removed inv_W_bits from compute runtime args. Compute kernel now only gets num_blocks.
reason: Scaler 1/W is now in the CB filled by reader, no post_reduce_op needed

summary: All CBs balanced after switching to scaler=1/W approach. Same sync as before, no post_reduce_op changes.

hypothesis: Output values are IDENTICAL between scaler=1.0+post_reduce and scaler=1/W approaches. This strongly suggests the kernel is NOT being recompiled - a cached version is being used. Need to clear kernel cache.

Identical output with two different scaler approaches could mean: (1) kernel cache issue, or (2) both approaches produce identical results on hardware because the bf16 reduce accumulation introduces the same error regardless of scaler value. Need to verify kernel is recompiled by making a distinctive change (e.g., skip the reduce entirely to output tilize-untilize passthrough).

approach: DIAGNOSTIC: replaced full compute with passthrough tilize+untilize to verify kernel recompilation
Restored full stage 2 compute kernel (reduce+sub+square) with scaler=1/W

reason: Hardware bf16 reduce accumulation introduces ~0.01 mean error amplified to 0.375 max diff after squaring. FP32 dest accumulation should match PyTorch float32 internal reduction precision.

details: fp32_dest_acc_en=True produces zeros in bottom half of output (DEST capacity halved breaks untilize). Max diff 9.0. Need different approach.

reason: fp32 mode halves DEST to 8 tiles, breaking untilize which needs 16 for full 32-row tile processing. Bottom half of output becomes zeros.

hypothesis: Need to verify where the error comes from. Will modify kernel to output centered values (x-mean) instead of squared, to isolate whether the error is in the mean computation or in the squaring. If centered values have small errors, squaring amplifies them. If centered values are way off, the mean itself is wrong.

approach: DIAGNOSTIC: skip square phase, output centered values (x-mean) directly to isolate error source
Re-added Phase 4 square with WaitAndPopPerTile policy, untilize from cb_squared(c_27) instead of cb_centered(c_26)

summary: All CBs balanced: c_0(Wt push/pop via tilize), c_8(1 persistent, popped at end), c_24(Wt push tilize, wait reduce NoPop, no-op sub, manual pop), c_25(1 push reduce, wait+pop sub), c_26(Wt push sub PerTile, wait+pop square PerTile), c_27(Wt push square PerTile, wait+pop untilize), c_16(Wt push untilize, wait+pop writer)

details: max_diff=0.375, full pipeline with square phase re-added, WaitAndPopPerTile for square

hypothesis: The 0.375 max diff is consistent across centered and squared outputs, indicating the error originates in the reduce_row mean computation or sub COL broadcast. Will add DPRINT to inspect intermediate CB values (tilized input, mean, centered output) to identify exactly where values diverge.

approach: Added DPRINT diagnostics to inspect cb_mean, cb_tilized, and cb_centered after reduce and sub phases for first block only

description: Add variance computation (reduce_row on squared centered), add epsilon+rsqrt, multiply centered by inv_std

change: Added eps_packed as runtime arg index 7 for reader kernel
reason: Reader needs eps_packed to fill epsilon CB c_9 via fill_with_val

approach: Added eps_packed RT arg at index 7, fill cb_eps with fill_with_val_bfloat16 before main loop, also read gamma_addr and beta_addr RT args for future use

Added phases 5-7: reduce_row(var) on c_27->c_25, add eps+rsqrt c_25+c_9->c_28 with SCALAR bcast, mul centered*inv_std c_26*c_28->c_24 with COL bcast. Changed square to WaitUpfrontNoPop to persist c_26. Untilize now from c_24 (normalized). Added cb_eps wait at startup.

cb_summary: c0:Wt/Wt c8:1/persist c9:1/persist c16:Wt/Wt c24:Wt(tilize)+Wt(norm)/Wt(pop)+Wt(untilize) c25:1(mean)+1(var)/1(sub)+1(add_eps) c26:Wt/Wt c27:Wt/Wt c28:1/1

details: rsqrt_tile_init and rsqrt_tile not declared in scope - missing include for SFPU ops

description: rsqrt_tile_init and rsqrt_tile not in scope because missing include for SFPU rsqrt header
evidence: compilation error: rsqrt_tile_init was not declared in this scope

change: Added #include api/compute/eltwise_unary/rsqrt.h for rsqrt_tile_init and rsqrt_tile declarations

upstream_fixes: Added eps_packed as reader RT arg index 7
design_deviations: Added rsqrt.h include not mentioned in design

description: Add gamma/beta reading and tilizing in reader, mul by gamma (ROW broadcast), add beta (ROW broadcast) in compute

approach: Added compile-time has_gamma/has_beta parsing via next_compile_time_args_offset. Added gamma/beta TensorAccessor setup with conditional CT arg indexing. Before main loop: if has_gamma read 1 gamma stick into c_0 push Wt pages, if has_beta read 1 beta stick into c_0 push Wt pages.

Added cb_gamma/cb_beta constants. Pre-loop: tilize gamma c_0->c_1 and beta c_0->c_2 with persistent wait. Main loop: Phase 8 mul gamma ROW bcast c_24*c_1->c_27, Phase 9 add beta ROW bcast with correct input/output routing. cb_pre_untilize determined at compile time from has_gamma/has_beta. Pop gamma/beta after loop.

cb_summary: c0:Wt(gamma)+Wt(beta)+Wt(input)/Wt+Wt+Wt c1:Wt/persist+popEnd c2:Wt/persist+popEnd c8:1/persist c9:1/persist c16:Wt/Wt c24:Wt+Wt(norm)+Wt(beta_out)/Wt+Wt(gamma)+Wt(untilize) c25:1+1/1+1 c26:Wt/Wt c27:Wt+Wt(gamma)/Wt+Wt(beta) c28:1/1

design_deviations: Used next_compile_time_args_offset for dynamic CT arg indexing in reader
